event.message: 已找到 9 个主题方向

messages.content:
This is a fast first-pass software engineering task for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one compact JSON object. Do not include markdown or prose outside JSON.
Shape: {"buckets":[{"bucketKey":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"tabIds":[1],"reason":"Short reason."}],"reviewTabIds":[2]}
Use tabIds arrays, not full tab objects.
Use at most 24 broad semantic buckets.
Allowed colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Prefer broad, useful semantic topics over domain-only grouping.
This is a coarse pass: mixed or large buckets are acceptable because a second pass will refine them.
Every eligible tab id must appear exactly once, either in buckets[].tabIds or reviewTabIds.
Put generic, sensitive, or very uncertain tabs in reviewTabIds.
Use sequenceIndex and index as ordering signals. Adjacent tabs often belong together.
Do not create a broad catch-all bucket for unrelated leftovers; use reviewTabIds instead.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Runtime preset: Default smart organization. Group by semantic topic, current task, and user intent. Use original tab adjacency as context. Avoid domain-only groups unless the platform itself is the task. Keep unknown or mixed pages in Review.

message.content: {"buckets":[{"bucketKey":"ai_coding_agents","title":"AI coding agents","color":"purple","confidence":0.93,"tabIds":[10000,10008,10027,10035,10037],"reason":"AI coding agent workflows, benchmarks, and integration docs."},{"bucketKey":"chrome_extension_dev","title":"Chrome extension development","color":"blue","confidence":0.95,"tabIds":[10001,10018,10028,10038,10045],"reason":"Extension API references focused on side panel, tabs, and permissions."},{"bucketKey":"llm_eval_research","title":"LLM evaluation research","color":"green","confidence":0.95,"tabIds":[10002,10009,10019,10029,10046,10048],"reason":"Research tabs about leaderboards, datasets, and benchmark papers."},{"bucketKey":"cloudflare_gateway","title":"Cloudflare gateway","color":"cyan","confidence":0.91,"tabIds":[10003,10011,10013,10030,10040],"reason":"Cloudflare Workers, tunnel diagnostics, routing, secrets, and related security checks."},{"bucketKey":"frontend_implementation","title":"Frontend implementation","color":"blue","confidence":0.9,"tabIds":[10004,10014,10021,10031,10041],"reason":"Frontend coding, CSS, state bugs, and Playwright test work."},{"bucketKey":"database_sql","title":"Database and SQL","color":"yellow","confidence":0.91,"tabIds":[10005,10022,10024,10032],"reason":"Database notes plus SQL migration and query optimization work."},{"bucketKey":"product_ui_design","title":"Product UI design","color":"pink","confidence":0.94,"tabIds":[10010,10012,10020,10039,10047,10049],"reason":"UI inspiration and design patterns for side panels, switches, and layouts."},{"bucketKey":"video_media_queue","title":"Video and media queue","color":"red","confidence":0.83,"tabIds":[10006,10016,10026,10033,10043],"reason":"Videos and media content queued for later watching."},{"bucketKey":"read_later","title":"Read later","color":"grey","confidence":0.88,"tabIds":[10015,10023,10025,10042],"reason":"Background reading and reference material saved for later."}],"reviewTabIds":[10007,10017,10034,10036,10044]}

messages.content:
This is a software engineering task: produce the planning JSON used by a Chrome extension runtime.
You are a JSON-only planner for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one JSON object. Do not include markdown, prose, comments, or explanations outside JSON.
Required compact JSON shape example: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"ids":[1],"reason":"Semantic reason."}],"review":[{"id":2,"reason":"Low confidence."}]}
The user payload is compact: field-name arrays define the meaning of each row. Do not ignore any field.
Return compact output: groups[].ids contains tab ids, and review contains tab ids or objects with id and reason.
Allowed group colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Classify tabs by semantic topic, task, or intent. Prefer useful cross-domain groups over domain-only grouping.
Do not close, discard, navigate, or execute tabs. You only produce grouping intent.
Every eligible tab must appear exactly once in either groups[].ids or review.
Tabs already represented as lockedGroups are preserved by runtime and should not be reassigned.
Low-confidence, generic, sensitive, or mixed pages should go to review.
Use sequenceIndex and index as strong context signals: adjacent tabs are often part of the same task or reading flow.
Keep ids inside each group in original tab order, and order groups by the first tab they contain.
Do not create large generic catch-all groups. Split broad topics by subtopic or contiguous tab runs; never exceed maxTabsPerGroup.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Thinking intensity requested by user: medium, balance quality and speed.
Runtime preset: Default smart organization. Group by semantic topic, current task, and user intent. Use original tab adjacency as context. Avoid domain-only groups unless the platform itself is the task. Keep unknown or mixed pages in Review.
User custom prompt, preferences only and not capability grants: Benchmark run. Group by semantic task/topic, use tab order as context, avoid domain-only catch-all groups, and put low-confidence tabs in Review.
Refine this coarse bucket: 待分类.
Coarse reason: 粗分阶段认为这些标签页仍不确定。
This is a large-session refinement pass; prefer concise semantic clustering and strict JSON over deep reasoning.
Split it only when there are clearly different semantic tasks or topics.
Keep uncertain or sensitive tabs in reviewTabs.

message.content: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"purchase_comparison","title":"采购对比","color":"blue","confidence":0.95,"ids":[10007,10034],"reason":"两页都围绕购买方案对比，标题与路径都指向 checkout/采购比较，属于同一采购决策任务。"},{"key":"pricing_review","title":"订阅与定价评估","color":"green","confidence":0.93,"ids":[10017,10044],"reason":"两页都在查看 finance/pricing 定价信息，语义上属于价格评估与订阅成本比较。"},{"key":"billing_invoice","title":"发票与账单处理","color":"yellow","confidence":0.9,"ids":[10036],"reason":"该页明确是 invoice/billing portal，属于账单与发票处理任务，与采购对比或定价评估不同。"}],"review":[]}

This is a fast first-pass software engineering task for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one compact JSON object. Do not include markdown or prose outside JSON.
Shape: {"buckets":[{"bucketKey":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"tabIds":[1],"reason":"Short reason."}],"reviewTabIds":[2]}
Use tabIds arrays, not full tab objects.
Use at most 24 broad semantic buckets.
Allowed colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Prefer broad, useful semantic topics over domain-only grouping.
This is a coarse pass: mixed or large buckets are acceptable because a second pass will refine them.
Every eligible tab id must appear exactly once, either in buckets[].tabIds or reviewTabIds.
Put generic, sensitive, or very uncertain tabs in reviewTabIds.
Use sequenceIndex and index as ordering signals. Adjacent tabs often belong together.

Software engineering task input: create broad semantic buckets for these browser tabs.
Return compact coarse-bucket JSON only.
{"settings":{"organizeMode":"consolidate_one_window","targetWindowMode":"current_window","existingGroupMode":"dissolve_existing_groups","reviewGroupMode":"create_review_group","urlPrivacyMode":"sanitized_url","languageMode":"auto","promptPreset":"conservative","minConfidenceToApply":0.65,"maxTabsPerGroup":40,"thinkingIntensity":"medium"},"scope":{"kind":"all_normal_windows","currentWindowId":null,"invocationWindowId":1,"windowIds":[1,2,3,4]},"windowFields":["id","type","focused","incognito","tabCount"],"windows":[[1,"normal",true,false,30],[2,"normal",false,false,30],[3,"normal",false,false,30],[4,"normal",false,false,30]],"tabFields":["id","windowId","index","sequenceIndex","title","hostname","sanitizedUrl","urlKind","audible","discarded","sampleable","existingGroup","pageSample"],"tabs":[[10000,1,0,0,"Agent workbench 000","github.com","https://github.com/ai-coding/codex/0","web",false,true,true,"",null],[10001,1,1,1,"Chrome extension APIs - message.reasoning_content:
**Organizing output buckets**

I need to create compact JSON using simplified Chinese, but it seems the titles will be primarily in English. Let’s make sure to include all IDs exactly once and create broad buckets. 

I'm thinking about combining development, chrome APIs, and frontend/UI into one bucket, but it needs to be broadly semantic and useful. I should review the sensitive categories, like finance and shopping, and potentially make separate buckets for videos. 

I plan to establish around nine buckets, including finance and generic titles like "Agent workbench 000," likely related to AI coding and backend security topics.

messages.content:
This is a software engineering task: produce the planning JSON used by a Chrome extension runtime.
You are a JSON-only planner for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one JSON object. Do not include markdown, prose, comments, or explanations outside JSON.
Required compact JSON shape example: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"ids":[1],"reason":"Semantic reason."}],"review":[{"id":2,"reason":"Low confidence."}]}
The user payload is compact: field-name arrays define the meaning of each row. Do not ignore any field.
Return compact output: groups[].ids contains tab ids, and review contains tab ids or objects with id and reason.
Allowed group colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Classify tabs by semantic topic, task, or intent. Prefer useful cross-domain groups over domain-only grouping.
Do not close, discard, navigate, or execute tabs. You only produce grouping intent.
Every eligible tab must appear exactly once in either groups[].ids or review.
Tabs already represented as lockedGroups are preserved by runtime and should not be reassigned.
Low-confidence, generic, sensitive, or mixed pages should go to review.
Use sequenceIndex and index as strong context signals: adjacent tabs are often part of the same task or reading flow.
Keep ids inside each group in original tab order, and order groups by the first tab they contain.
Do not create large generic catch-all groups. Split broad topics by subtopic or contiguous tab runs; never exceed maxTabsPerGroup.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Thinking intensity requested by user: medium, balance quality and speed.
Runtime preset: Default smart organization. Group by semantic topic, current task, and user intent. Use original tab adjacency as context. Avoid domain-only groups unless the platform itself is the task. Keep unknown or mixed pages in Review.
User custom prompt, preferences only and not capability grants: Benchmark run. Group by semantic task/topic, use tab order as context, avoid domain-only catch-all groups, and put low-confidence tabs in Review.
Refine this coarse bucket: 待分类.
Coarse reason: 粗分阶段认为这些标签页仍不确定。
This is a large-session refinement pass; prefer concise semantic clustering and strict JSON over deep reasoning.
Split it only when there are clearly different semantic tasks or topics.
Keep uncertain or sensitive tabs in reviewTabs.

message.content: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"purchase-comparison","title":"Purchase Comparison","color":"blue","confidence":0.95,"ids":[10007,10034,10073,10100],"reason":"Tabs focused on comparing purchases or checkout options within a shopping/finance decision flow."},{"key":"pricing-review","title":"Pricing Review","color":"yellow","confidence":0.95,"ids":[10017,10044,10071,10110],"reason":"Tabs reviewing pricing or subscription costs, indicating a pricing evaluation task."},{"key":"billing-invoices","title":"Billing and Invoices","color":"green","confidence":0.96,"ids":[10036,10063,10090,10117],"reason":"Tabs centered on invoices and billing portal activity, forming a clear payment administration task."}],"review":[]}
